Market Analysis: • COHR's long-term trend remains powerfully bullish with all moving averages rising in perfect hierarchical alignment.
• Short-term momentum is fading rapidly, signaled by a contracting MACD histogram, cooling RSI, and bearish volume patterns.
• A correction toward the 10-day EMA or 50-day SMA is likely, favoring a hold on existing positions and patience on new entries.
Social Sentiment: • Strong Q3 earnings and 40%+ datacenter growth reinforced COHR’s bull case, anchored by the NVIDIA partnership and a record backlog extending into 2028.
• Valuation anxiety surged as a 183x trailing P/E, DCF estimates ~25% below price, and zero insider buying signaled the stock may be overvalued.
• The stock’s 5.5% Friday sell-off broke momentum, shifting sentiment cautious with $350–$370 emerging as a potential re-entry zone.
News Analysis: • COHR posted a strong Q3 beat with datacenter revenue surging to 75% of sales and raised Q4 guidance above consensus.
• NVIDIA’s $2B investment and supply lock-in solidify COHR’s competitive moat in AI optical interconnects.
• Sticky inflation, Fed uncertainty, and energy shocks create macro headwinds that threaten COHR’s high valuation multiple.
Fundamentals: • Coherent demonstrates powerful operational momentum with eight consecutive quarters of revenue growth and expanding gross margins.
• The latest quarter's negative free cash flow of $537 million and surging inventory levels raise significant working capital concerns.
• Extreme trailing valuation and a stock price above analyst consensus targets suggest limited upside and elevated risk.
Final Decision: • COHR is rated Underweight and requires an immediate one-third position reduction, not a full exit.
• Near-term risk is elevated due to technical exhaustion, fragile free cash flow, and macro sensitivity for high-beta stocks.
• The long-term AI thesis remains intact with disciplined re-entry triggers set around a 50-day SMA test or upside invalidation above $413.
